title: On-chain 101: exchange inflows & outflows
_note: EVERGREEN (warm-up bank for ad traffic). No invented numbers/prices/dates.
body:
⛓️ On-chain 101 — exchange inflows & outflows, and what they hint

What it is
Every coin sitting on an exchange wallet can be sold instantly. Coins held in private wallets can't. On-chain flow data (Glassnode-style) tracks coins moving TO and OFF exchanges — and that movement is a clue about intent.

The two directions
• Coins moving TO exchanges → supply lining up where it can be sold. This is sell-pressure CONTEXT. Big holders don't move coins onto an exchange to admire them.
• Coins moving OFF exchanges → supply going into cold storage, off the market. This is accumulation CONTEXT — coins being parked, not prepped for sale.

Why we care
Flows are a read on positioning that price alone hides. A rally while coins quietly drain off exchanges looks very different from a rally while coins flood ON. The first has supply tightening underneath it; the second has supply waiting to hit the bid.

The big caveat
This is CONTEXT, never a standalone signal. Flows can be internal exchange shuffles, custody migrations, or wallet relabels that look dramatic and mean nothing. Reacting to a single spike is how you get faked out.

How we use it
• We treat flows as a background lean, not a trigger.
• We always cross-check against funding, OI and the liquidation heatmap.
• If on-chain whispers one direction but our other reads disagree, the flow data loses — confluence wins.

No single number gets you into a trade here. On-chain is one voice in the room. It only matters when it agrees with the others — that's the ≥2-confirmation rule behind every #Signal.

#CryptoSchool
